steps.title: Set up the inequality test
steps.content: For each $(x, y)$ pair to satisfy $y > 7x - 4$, we substitute and verify. A table qualifies only if all its rows pass.

steps.title: Backsolve the answer choices
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (x = 3, y = 17):
$7(3) - 4 = 17$. Is \$17 > 17$? No.

Fails. Testing choice C (x = 3, y = 21):
$7(3) - 4 = 17$. Is \$21 > 17$? Yes. Checking row 2: $x = 5, y = 27$ and $7(5) - 4 = 31$. Is \$27 > 31$? No.

Fails. Testing choice A (x = 3, y = 13):
$7(3) - 4 = 17$. Is \$13 > 17$? No.

Fails. Testing choice D (all three rows):
  • Row 1: $x = 3, y = 21$ and $7(3) - 4 = 17$. Is \$21 > 17$? Yes. ✓
  • Row 2: $x = 5, y = 35$ and $7(5) - 4 = 31$. Is \$35 > 31$? Yes. ✓
  • Row 3: $x = 8, y = 56$ and $7(8) - 4 = 52$. Is \$56 > 52$? Yes. ✓
All rows satisfy the inequality. That matches choice D — D is correct.
